stories.title: As a Care Partner, I can create an Assessment Request
stories.description: Care Partners need to initiate the assessment process by creating Assessment Requests for packages, selecting assessment types, and choosing onboarded suppliers (for OT assessments). This triggers the secure link generation and assessor notification workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: CP can select assessment type (OT, Physio, Nurse, Contractor) when creating request
CP sees list of onboarded suppliers filtered by assessment type (for OT only)
System generates secure link and creates Assessment object on submission
Supplier receives notification with secure link
Dashboard shows Assessment Request with status tracking
CP can create multiple Assessment Requests with different types for same package

stories.title: As an Assessor, I can upload assessment documents
stories.description: Assessors need to upload assessment documents (PDFs, images) through a secure authenticated workflow so the system can extract ATHM information. This digitizes assessment evidence and enables downstream AI extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Assessor can log in using existing Portal credentials via secure link
System handles account creation for unregistered assessors
Assessor can upload multiple files (PDFs and images)
System validates file size and format before accepting
System captures metadata (uploader, timestamp, document type, package)
System auto-classifies document type (report/quote/amendment) with manual override option

stories.title: As the System, I can extract and classify ATHM items
stories.description: The system needs to automatically extract product/modification details from uploaded documents and suggest Tier-5/Tier-3 classifications with pathway assignments (Low Risk/Advice/Prescribed). This reduces manual workload and enables efficient recommendation review.
acceptanceCriteria: System extracts individual ATHM items with product/modification details
System suggests Tier-5 classification for products with confidence score
System suggests Tier-3 classification for services with confidence score
System assigns pathway classification (Low Risk, Advice, Prescribed)
Raw extraction output preserved in audit trail
System allows manual entry with warning when Tier-5 dataset unavailable

stories.title: As an Assessor, I can review and confirm recommendations
stories.description: Assessors need to review AI-extracted recommendations, edit Tier codes using inline search, and confirm all items before submission. This mandatory human-in-the-loop ensures data quality and prevents submission of unconfirmed items.
acceptanceCriteria: Assessor can edit Tier-5 codes using inline search and filtering
Visual indicators clearly show low-confidence extractions
System logs all edits in audit trail
Assessor can confirm each recommendation individually
Validation prevents submission when not all recommendations confirmed
CP receives automatic notification on submission
Submissions become immutable after completion

stories.title: As a Care Partner, I can review and accept recommendations
stories.description: Care Partners need to review submitted recommendations from all Assessments, view aggregated recommendations, and accept individual items to control budget allocation. Acceptance triggers Inclusion Seed creation for Advice/Prescribed pathways.
acceptanceCriteria: CP can view all submitted recommendations with Tier-5 classifications and evidence links
CP sees aggregated view of Recommendations from all Assessments for an Assessment Request
CP can view and reconcile duplicate recommendations from multiple assessors
CP can accept individual recommendations (no reject action)
Recommendations remain visible with 'Accepted' status after acceptance
System creates Inclusion Seed for Advice/Prescribed pathways (not Low Risk)
Ignored recommendations remain in 'Submitted' state for later acceptance

stories.title: As a Care Partner, I can add recommendations to Service Plan
stories.description: Care Partners need to add accepted recommendations to the Service Plan (budget) with terminal tier values prefilled. This enables funding allocation while maintaining bidirectional links between Service Plan items and source assessments.
acceptanceCriteria: CP can use 'Add Service' action on accepted recommendations
System prefills only Tier-5 value for products or Tier-3 value for services
CP must enter description, quantity, cost following normal budget flow
System maintains bidirectional links (Service Plan Item ↔ Assessment ↔ Recommendation)
Source recommendation preserved when Service Plan item edited/removed
CP can view source assessment and recommendation IDs from Service Plan item

stories.title: As a Billing Processor, I can view Tier context on invoices
stories.description: Billing Processors need to view Tier-5/Tier-3 context on Service Plan items and invoices to manually match invoices to budget lines. This improves billing efficiency without implementing automated matching logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Tier-5 or Tier-3 values clearly displayed on Service Plan items
Invoice AI classifies uploaded invoice line items with Tier-5 values
Billing processors can view tier-coded items in existing Invoice UI (no new screen)
Tier context helps identify correct budget line when matching invoices
No auto-gating or auto-release logic (context only)

stories.title: As Compliance, I can access comprehensive audit logs
stories.description: Compliance users need comprehensive immutable audit logging across all state changes (uploads, edits, confirmations, submissions, acceptances) with export capabilities (CSV/JSON) and filtering by package, user, action type, and date range.
acceptanceCriteria: All actions create immutable audit log entries
Audit logs can be filtered by package, user, action type, date range
Audit log entries show before/after state for modifications
Audit logs can be exported in standard formats (CSV, JSON)
System prevents modification of audit log entries (immutable)

stories.title: As a System Owner, I can manage Tier-5 dataset versions
stories.description: System Owners need a canonical versioned Tier-5 dataset to ensure consistent classification across all assessments. Historical classifications must remain valid after dataset updates, with graceful degradation when dataset unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: Assessors can search current versioned Tier-3 → Tier-4 → Tier-5 hierarchy
Assessors can view classification rules and descriptions
Historical classifications remain valid when dataset updated
System allows manual entry with warning when dataset unavailable
Historical recommendations show which dataset version was used
